notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
support model counts one beneficiary and active-ability heals/shields only (passive heals, CC, and damage-reduction utility are not scored)
the sim opens on Oblivion Crown, which the field rarely builds (1.1% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below

coachLine: Sim-optimal for heal/shield output: first spike lands around minute 15 (Oblivion Crown), third item by minute 28. Take Vesh: +12.8% on the damage rotation at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Sacrosanct
augments.note: the free Consecrated Ground is credited at its rank-5 stated heal (140 +20% MP); assumes Q maxed by the time R matters

augments.name: Circuit Reset
augments.note: reduces the shielded ALLY's cooldowns; team-side value outside the single-hero sim

augments.name: Essence Nullifier
augments.note: drains and converts target mana; target resource state unknown

augments.name: Aerial Assistance
augments.note: new 1.15 hero augment; effect not yet modeled by the sim

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 91 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 719 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +43% more one-combo burst: try Oblivion Crown over Marshal (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 91 auto-attack DPS in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Lunaria: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite, at the cost of 9% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Lunaria: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite, at the cost of 9% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Lunaria: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite, at the cost of 8% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Lunaria: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite, at the cost of 8% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Lunaria: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite, at the cost of 8% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Lunaria: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite, at the cost of 9% of this kit's damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Sacrosanct” ⇒ sustain; the sim models this augment — field support 50.7% over 1,744 games.
